title: Inflation rate hit 3.0% in September, lower than expected, long-awaited CPI report shows - CNBC
query: [SOURCES] "Federal Reserve" AND (emergency OR surprise OR pivot)
reasoning: CPI beat (0.3% vs 0.4% expected; 3.0% vs 3.1% expected) with core CPI also beating (0.2% vs 0.3% expected; 3.0% vs 3.1% expected). Historical table shows CPI misses (below consensus) produced pct_1h range of -0.019% to +0.019% with mixed follow-through, but this is a MISS (softer than expected), which aligns with Example 11 (October 2023 CPI below consensus: -0.247% pct_15m, criticality high). Article explicitly states 'keeps the door wide open for another interest rate cut next week' and analyst commentary confirms dovish market reaction. Tier 1 primary structural event with clear surprise magnitude and policy implication.
direction_chain: Article reports CPI and core CPI both missed consensus (came in softer: 0.3% vs 0.4% monthly, 3.0% vs 3.1% annual) → Historical table shows CPI misses (2025-03-11, 2025-03-12) produced pct_1h of -0.019% to +0.019% with 1d follow-through of +0.116% to +0.126%, but Example 11 (October 2023 CPI below consensus) showed -0.247% pct_15m with downward DXY pressure → Softer inflation reduces Fed hiking pressure, supports rate cut next week, weakens USD relative to other G10 currencies → DXY down.
